channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Data reveals V/Line punctuality on two routes dropped to lowest in a decade
item.description: The latest V/Line data reveals only 56 per cent of services arrived on time on two regional train routes during Victoria's first month of free public transport.
item.dc:creator: Nethma Dandeniya and Philippe Perez

media:group.media:description: V/Line's punctuality was lower on the majority of regional routes across Victoria's first month of free public transport, as compared to March 2026.

item.title: LA suburb evacuated over fears chemical tank could explode
item.description: Evacuation orders are in place for an area covering tens of thousands of people in a suburb of Los Angeles.

media:group.media:description: Evacuation orders were issued for 40,000 people, and several shelters remained open, including at three high schools.

item.title: Breaking: White House lawn cleared after apparent gunshots
item.description: Reporters were told to sprint into the Press Briefing Room after gunshots were heard nearby.

media:group.media:description: Police and members of the Secret Service block streets around the White House.

item.title: Meet the groundskeeper breathing life into a regional war cemetery
item.description: Tending to the living to honour the dead — that's what the dedicated groundskeeper of the Adelaide River War Cemetery says has been the pinnacle of his gardening career.
item.dc:creator: Lillian Watkins

media:group.media:description: Rob Thorne is the dedicated groundskeeper who keeps the Adelaide River War Cemetery alive.

item.title: NASCAR champion's cause of death confirmed by his family
item.description: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch died from severe pneumonia that turned into sepsis, according to a statement provided by his family on Saturday.

media:group.media:description: NASCAR is in mourning over the sudden passing of two-time Cup series champion Kyle Busch.

item.title: Autumn rain in SA brings 'incredible' boom in birdlife
item.description: Parts of South Australia are preparing for an exceptional year as autumn rain brings an increase in birdlife.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Pearce

media:group.media:description: Inland Australia is experiencing a boom in birdlife after heavy rain.

item.title: Multiple safety violations in China mine explosion, investigation finds
item.description: An explosion at a coal mine in China that killed at least 82 people was the result of the company involved having committed multiple serious safety violations, an early investigation has found.

media:group.media:description: Rescue workers prepare to descend into a coal mine in the aftermath of the explosion.

item.title: 'There's one kid in every classroom': Grief program teaches families to heal
item.description: One in 20 Australians under the age of 18 has lost a parent, but families say grief services for children are limited. A national program teaching children how to navigate grief is trying to change that.
item.dc:creator: Meg Whitfield

media:group.media:description: Angela says "the single biggest help" has been meeting other people with similar experiences.

item.title: Deadly walk framed as a 'step count' boost in Tourism Australia post
item.description: It looks beautiful in the Instagram post with blue skies and picturesque tarns carved by glaciers. But read further and the multi-day Tasmanian hike is considered one of Australia's most challenging bushwalks. In fact, it can be deadly.
item.dc:creator: Crystal McKay

media:group.media:description: The technically challenging walk takes in sights such as Procyon Peak.

item.title: Pipes, posters and packaging show Australia's history of smoking
item.description: Smoking artefacts from as far back as the early 1900s show the Far North Coast how accepted smoking once was.
item.dc:creator: Lachie McLeod

media:group.media:description: John Sim shows postcards from smoking packets, part of the Up In Smoke Exhibition on at Crawford House.

item.title: Man allegedly armed with gun shot dead by police north of Brisbane
item.description: Queensland Police said the man approached officers and made threats towards police whilst armed with a firearm, and as a result was shot.

media:group.media:description: Cavill Way, Narangba, was closed off on Sunday following a police involved shooting.

item.title: Mystery billboard bites back at critics of Olympic rowing location
item.description: Amid months of debate about the location for the 2032 Olympic rowing events, a billboard came to life. Who put it there and why was a mystery until now.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Beavan

media:group.media:description: The billboard has been up in Rockhampton for several months and alternates between designs.

item.title: Vingegaard takes control of Giro as Aussie Hindley makes move
item.description: Giro d'Italia favourite Jonas Vingegaard has taken the leader's pink jersey with his third stage win of the race while Australia's former champ Jai Hindley made a significant move towards the podium in Pila.

media:group.media:description: Jai Hindley was third on stage 14 of the Giro d'Italia, moving up to fifth overall in the race.

item.title: 'I've had enough': Why this loyal Labor voter is switching to One Nation
item.description: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".
item.dc:creator: Nick Dole

media:group.media:description: Silvana Rota has turned her back on the Labor Party in favour of One Nation.

item.title: When a phantom killed Dawn Hillier, he returned the knife to her kitchen and vanished
item.description: Teenage Dawn Hillier was a "ray of sunshine". Almost 50 years later, years of investigation, a coronial inquiry, and a big reward have been unable to solve her brutal murder.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Jones

media:group.media:description: A reward remains on offer for anyone with information about the 1977 murder of Dawn Hillier at Gordon Park.

item.title: From farm gate to stage door, theatre is a release from life on the land
item.description: Putting their livestock, tractors and drought feeding aside for a night, these farmers are taking to the stage.
item.dc:creator: Emily Middleton

media:group.media:description: Jack Dodds has written and co-directed a number of musicals that have toured the country.

item.title: 'An absolutely pleasant surprise': Why single mums are moving in together
item.description: As rental and living costs surge, and as single mums remain one of the most vulnerable groups in the country, some are moving in together and finding many benefits.
item.dc:creator: James Oaten

media:group.media:description: Hayley Grimmond (left), Anja Homburg and their daughters have become housemates.

item.title: Socceroos duo's Premier League dream dashed as Hull beat Middlesbrough
item.description: Middlesbrough's two Socceroos, Riley McGree and Sammy Silvera, have been left shattered after all the drama surrounding the Championship play-offs ended with a deflating, last-gasp 1-0 defeat to Premier League-bound Hull City at Wembley.

media:group.media:description: Socceroo Riley McGree (right) played 76 minutes in Middlesbrough's Championship playoff final defeat to Hull City.

item.title: Plagued by 18 months of blackouts, locals say $10m fix is long overdue
item.description: After losing hundreds of thousands of dollars due to power outages, Yorke Peninsula businesses say SA Power Networks' $10 million action plan should have come a long time ago.
item.dc:creator: Arj Ganesan

media:group.media:description: Josh Parsons said there were times when the brewery was having power outages "10 times a night".

item.title: Mercedes duo clash as Piastri pulls off stunning overtake on Hamilton
item.description: Mercedes driver George Russell has enjoyed a stellar day in Canada with victory in the sprint race and qualifying on pole for the grand prix, but suffered the season's first flashpoint with teammate and championship rival Kimi Antonelli.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Kimi Antonelli goes off track, while teammate George Russell holds the lead.

item.title: Iran war lays bare overlooked holes in Australia's sovereign capability
item.description: The Iran war has not only highlighted the vulnerability of global supply chains — it has also exposed the overlooked parts of Australia's manufacturing sector needed in a crisis.
item.dc:creator: Megan Hughes

media:group.media:description: The Australian Industry Group says food production relies on access to PPE and plastic packaging.

item.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question
item.description: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Thorpe

media:group.media:description: A deep-sea rescue diver, trailing a guide line, explores the inner sections of a cave in the Maldives where five Italians died.

item.title: Trump says agreement with Iran over end of war is 'largely negotiated'
item.description: US President Donald Trump ​says a memorandum of understanding ​on ‌a peace deal with Iran that would open the Strait of Hormuz has been "largely negotiated" and will be revealed soon, however Iran has sown doubt as to how far-reaching the agreement will be.

media:group.media:description: US President Donald Trump speaks to reporters over the North Atlantic as he returns to Washington from the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, aboard Air Force One, US, January 22, 2026.

item.title: 'Midwits' and media bust-ups herald One Nation's arrival in SA parliament
item.description: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall

media:group.media:description: One Nation state leader Cory Bernardi and federal leader Pauline Hanson the opening of South Australia's new parliamentary term earlier this month.

item.title: This private island paradise has a bargain price tag, and problems galore
item.description: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.
item.dc:creator: Riley Stuart

media:group.media:description: Makri Island is a slice of paradise with plenty of problems.
